nodes.label: TraceSink port
nodes.rationale: Hexagonal sink port keeps the MLflow SDK out of the core; CompletionService builds a pure TraceRecord and hands it to the sink. Backend kept general via the port but only an MLflow adapter is built.

nodes.label: MLflowTraceSink adapter
nodes.rationale: Uses MlflowClient with an explicit experiment_id per trace instead of autolog/global set_experiment, which is race-prone in an async multi-tenant server. Fail-safe: sink exceptions are logged and swallowed, never propagated to the client. v1 is metadata-only (no payloads).

nodes.label: NullTraceSink (no-op)
nodes.rationale: Unset MLFLOW_TRACKING_URI means observability disabled: dev and tests run without MLflow.

nodes.label: TraceDispatcher (bounded queue + worker)
nodes.rationale: Traces are written off the hot path via a bounded queue and worker thread (replacing the originally sketched per-response BackgroundTask); blocking MLflow HTTP never runs on the event loop, and MLflow being down never blocks startup or requests.

nodes.label: MetricsAggregator + MlflowMetricsPublisher
nodes.rationale: Fleet-level ops metrics (requests/errors, latency, tokens, cost, dropped traces) logged every MLFLOW_METRICS_INTERVAL seconds to a long-lived gateway-metrics-<host> run, charted natively by the MLflow UI.
